title: Chapter 270
content:

The flashes of memory of training with Xenovia kept coming back to him as he watched Bela. The firm stance, the precise movements, the overwhelming strength. She was just as tireless.

"If Bela is like this... I don't even want to imagine how Scarlet fights..." thought Strax, wiping the sweat dripping from his forehead with his arm.

Discouragement began to weigh heavily on his chest. Ever since he'd arrived in this place, he'd felt that all his efforts had seemed pointless. No matter how hard he tried, it seemed impossible to overcome those vampires. It was as if each one was an insurmountable obstacle, a wall he could never climb.

"What happened to her?" Daniela asked, her voice full of surprise and a touch of concern.

She was looking at Bela, who was lying on the floor, completely motionless. It was the first time she had seen her sister in that state - without energy, vulnerable, even sweating. For Daniela, this was unprecedented and absurd.

"I don't know... She must be tired," replied Strax, still trying to catch his breath.

"You two look like the walking dead," commented Cassandra, suddenly appearing and watching the scene curiously.

Daniela frowned, leaning over slightly to call out to her sister. "Belatrix?" She used her full name, something that made Strax raise his eyebrows. Until that moment, he hadn't known that Bela had such a formal name.

"Are you all right, sister?" Daniela kept calling.

She insisted, repeating the call several times, but Bela didn't react.

"..."

Nothing. Bela continued to stare at nothing, her expression empty and lifeless.

"Huh? Nothing! I just... I hit her a few times while we were practicing with wooden swords," he replied, confused.

Strax was even more confused, but answered honestly. "I guess so? I don't think much about where I'm going to hit when I'm fighting."

Another meaningful look between the sisters.

"Cassandra? What are you thinking of doing?" he asked, but she ignored him.
